title: 控方证人
summary:
In a room where Raul is bound by Madame de Winter, Mrs. Exe attempts to reach a child. Raul, terrified and helpless, commands her to sit down to protect the medium, but Madame de Winter ignores him, claiming the child is her own flesh and blood. Ayesha snatches the child into her arms, followed by an anguished wail and a crunching sound from behind a curtain. Madame Exe runs downstairs to unlock a door. Raul manages to break free from his ropes just as Iris arrives. Together, they pull back the curtain to find a scene of blood; Madame is dead and appears to have shrunk to half her original size. Elise questions the cause of this phenomenon, but Raul, distraught, can only scream in confusion.

In a different setting, the Dinsmoor family prepares a dinner of eggs, beef, bread, and cheese. Mr. Dinsmoor, a burly man, maintains a dismissive attitude toward his daughter Margaret’s fears of ghosts, asserting that miracles do not happen. During the meal, which includes his wife and children Johnny and Margaret, a sudden knock at the door interrupts them.

Mortimer Cleveland, a researcher in psychosciences and parapsychology, has become stranded in the Wiltshire hills after his car tires were punctured in a storm. Seeking refuge, he follows flickering lights to a small house. Upon knocking, he hears whispers inside before a fifteen-year-old boy opens the door. Cleveland observes the family inside, noting the robust father, the pale, frightened mother, and a surprised girl at the table.
